HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUZANNE AGASI'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is granted. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. TIMOTHY B. BRODERICK of Broderick Law Firm, Tel No. (415) 678-9938 appearing for Plaintiff Suzanne Agasi;
Defendant/Judgment Debtor MICHELLE TON-NU WAYLAND, Tel No. (415) 678-9938 appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling with the sole modification that the motion is denied without prejudice. PLAINTIFF SUZANNE AGASI'S Motion For Assignment Order and For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor is denied without prejudice. The Court has broad discretion in determining whether to order an assignment, and may consider all relevant factors, including the reasonable economic needs of a natural person judgment debtor. (CCP 708.510(c).) Based on the evidence submitted with the Judgment Creditor's declaration, all of the rent collectable by the Judgment Creditor is necessary for Creditor to meet her reasonable economic needs. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
